message:
perf(fcp-mesh): cache IBLT sketch incrementally across gossip reconciliation (br-m68xt)

Closes flywheel_connectors-m68xt (PERF/P2).

Profiling found that GossipState::reconcile_with_peer_iblt() called
build_iblt() on every reconciliation, and build_iblt() re-hashed every
admitted object into a fresh IBLT before the subtract. One gossip
fanout round across P peers cost O(P * N) CPU + allocation even when
the local set hadn't changed between peers.

Fix: maintain the reconciliation IBLT incrementally.

- GossipState gains a `cached_iblt: Iblt` field, sized at construction
  via `Iblt::recommended_cell_count(config.reconciliation_batch_size)`.
- `announce_object` inserts into `cached_iblt` in O(1) — guarded by
  the existing `BTreeSet::insert` return-bool so we never double-insert.
- `remove_object` deletes from `cached_iblt` in O(1) — ONLY when
  `BTreeSet::remove` reports the id was present, so a spurious
  remove-twice can't push cell counters negative and corrupt decode.
- `build_iblt(expected_difference)` fast path: when the requested
  cell count matches the cached one, clone the cached IBLT (O(cell_count))
  instead of rebuilding (O(N)). For the hot path used by
  reconcile_with_peer_iblt during fanout — where callers consistently
  pass `reconciliation_batch_size` — this is the common case.
- `build_iblt` slow path: when the requested expected_difference maps
  to a different cell budget, fall back to the legacy full-rebuild.
  Preserves exact per-call semantics for callers that pass
  non-standard sizes.

Net: O(P * N) → O(P * cell_count) on the hot path, where cell_count is
typically << N after a few announces. Cache hit path is cell-count
memcpy; cache is never stale because the insert/delete updates piggy-
back on the authoritative admitted_objects mutations.

Regression tests:
- gossip_state_cached_iblt_stays_in_sync_with_admitted_objects: spins
  through 16 announces + every-other remove + a duplicate-remove,
  asserting the cached IBLT equals a freshly-rebuilt IBLT at EVERY
  step. Pins the incremental-maintenance invariant so a future
  refactor can't silently desync the cache (the Iblt API lacks a
  "counters all zero" assertion; per-step byte-identity is the
  strongest available structural check).
- gossip_state_build_iblt_slow_path_matches_admitted_set: forces the
  cell-count mismatch branch by passing expected_difference *= 4 + 7,
  asserts the slow path still produces a byte-identical rebuild. The
  fast-path short-circuit cannot mask slow-path semantics.

Added test-only accessor `admitted_objects_iter_for_test` on
GossipState so the tests can independently rebuild an IBLT without
needing the pub API surface to grow.

Verified:
- cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib gossip:: — 137/137 pass.
- cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib — 1197 pass, same 8 pre-existing
  failures from the parallel fcp-mesh emcdd / zone-fail-closed work
  (baseline-equivalent to the rp0ej state). None of the failures are
  in gossip::.

GossipState is `#[derive(Debug, Clone)]` with no Serialize/Deserialize;
adding the cached_iblt field does not affect wire format.

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
